card.intuition: Fast pointer moves twice as fast as slow — if there's a cycle, fast will lap slow inside it; if not, fast falls off the end.
card.analogy: Two runners on a track: if the track is circular, the faster runner will eventually catch up to the slower one. If the track is straight, the faster runner finishes first and never meets the slower one again.
card.space_complexity: O(1) — Floyd's algorithm needs no extra storage
when_to_use: Detect whether a linked list has a cycle
Find the node where the cycle begins (reset one pointer to head after meeting, advance both by 1)
Find the length of the cycle (count steps from meeting point back to itself)
Detect cycles in any sequence defined by a next-state function (e.g., rho algorithm for factoring)
gotchas: Check `fast and fast.next` before advancing fast — dereferencing fast.next when fast is None is a runtime error
Meeting point is NOT the cycle start — to find the start, move one pointer to head and advance both one step at a time; they meet at the entry
A hash-set approach is O(n) time and O(n) space — Floyd's is always preferred unless you need the actual nodes visited

exercise.prompt: Detect whether a linked list has a cycle using Floyd's fast/slow pointer algorithm.
hints: Fast moves two steps per iteration: fast = fast.next.next
If fast and slow ever point to the same node, a cycle exists — check with `is` (identity), not `==` (value equality)
exercise.test_code:
class ListNode:
    def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
        self.val=val; self.next=next
# No cycle
def make(vs):
    d=ListNode(); c=d
    for v in vs: c.next=ListNode(v); c=c.next
    return d.next
assert has_cycle(make([1,2,3,4])) == False, 'No cycle'
assert has_cycle(make([])) == False, 'Empty list'
assert has_cycle(make([1])) == False, 'Single node, no cycle'
# With cycle: 1->2->3->4->2
n1=ListNode(1); n2=ListNode(2); n3=ListNode(3); n4=ListNode(4)
n1.next=n2; n2.next=n3; n3.next=n4; n4.next=n2
assert has_cycle(n1) == True, 'Cycle at node 2'
# Self-loop
n=ListNode(1); n.next=n
assert has_cycle(n) == True, 'Self-loop'
print('PASS')
